title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Create virtual environment and install required packages for LayerSelect experiments. Includes PyTorch, transformers, datasets, and optimization libraries.

steps.step1: Create virtual environment: python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install PyTorch 2.4+ with CUDA: pip install torch==2.4.0 torchvision --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121
steps.step3: Install transformers and related: pip install transformers==4.45.0 accelerate==0.34.0 sentencepiece protobuf
steps.step4: Install datasets and evaluation: pip install datasets==2.21.0 evaluate==0.4.3 scikit-learn scipy
steps.step5: Install optimization libraries: pip install pymoo==0.6.1  # NSGA-II implementation
steps.step6: Install visualization: pip install matplotlib seaborn pandas

title: Download and Prepare Datasets
description: Download RULER benchmark (subset for 4K/8K/16K), calibration data for profiling, and prepare evaluation datasets. Use smaller subsets to fit time budget.

steps.step1: Download RULER benchmark: git clone https://github.com/hsiehjackson/RULER.git data/ruler
steps.step2: Generate RULER subsets with seq lengths 4096, 8192: python scripts/prepare_ruler.py --lengths 4096 8192 --output_dir data/ruler_processed
steps.step3: Download calibration data: Use RedPajama-Data-1T-Sample (1000 samples): wget -P data/calibration https://huggingface.co/datasets/togethercomputer/RedPajama-Data-1T-Sample/resolve/main/train.json
steps.step4: Preprocess calibration data: python scripts/preprocess_calibration.py --input data/calibration/train.json --output data/calibration/calibration_1k.jsonl --n_samples 256 --seq_len 4096
steps.step5: Download LongBench subset: Use THUDM/LongBench with needle-in-haystack and QA tasks: python scripts/download_longbench.py --tasks niah_single_1 niah_multikey_1 qasper --output_dir data/longbench
steps.step6: Verify data integrity: python scripts/verify_data.py --data_dir data/
steps.step7: Create dataset statistics: python scripts/dataset_stats.py > data/dataset_statistics.json

title: Download Pretrained Models
description: Download and cache pretrained models. Focus on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct as primary model due to time constraints.

steps.step1: Download Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct: python -c "from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM; AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained('meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct', cache_dir='models/')"
steps.step2: Download Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct (for cross-model validation): python -c "from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM; AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained('Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct', cache_dir='models/')"
steps.step3: Verify models fit in memory: Check 8B models use ~16GB in FP16, leaving room for KV cache
steps.step4: Create model configs: python scripts/create_model_configs.py --models meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct --output configs/models.yaml

notes: Using 2 models instead of 3 to fit time budget. Mistral-7B deferred to future work.

title: Baseline 1 - Full KV Cache (No Compression)
description: Evaluate models with full KV cache to establish upper bound. This is the simple baseline showing uncompressed performance.

steps.step1: Implement full KV cache inference: Create exp/baseline_full/run.py with standard transformer inference
steps.step2: Run Llama-3.1-8B on RULER 4K: CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python exp/baseline_full/run.py --model meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct --benchmark ruler --seq_len 4096 --seed 42 --output exp/baseline_full/ruler_4k_seed42.json
steps.step3: Run Llama-3.1-8B on RULER 8K: CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python exp/baseline_full/run.py --model meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct --benchmark ruler --seq_len 8192 --seed 42 --output exp/baseline_full/ruler_8k_seed42.json
steps.step4: Run LongBench evaluation: CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python exp/baseline_full/run.py --model meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct --benchmark longbench --seed 42 --output exp/baseline_full/longbench_seed42.json
steps.step5: Repeat with seed 43, 44: Run same commands with --seed 43 and --seed 44 for statistical significance
steps.step6: Aggregate results: python scripts/aggregate_results.py --input_dir exp/baseline_full/ --output exp/baseline_full/results.json

title: Baseline 2 - CLA (Uniform Grouping)
description: Implement and evaluate Cross-Layer Attention-style uniform grouping. Groups adjacent layers to share KV caches. Simple baseline showing fixed-pattern performance.

steps.step1: Implement CLA-style sharing: Create exp/baseline_cla/cla_sharing.py with uniform grouping (layer i shares with layer floor(i/group_size))
steps.step2: Run CLA-2 on Llama-3.1-8B (2x compression): CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python exp/baseline_cla/run.py --model meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct --group_size 2 --benchmark ruler --seq_len 4096 --seed 42 --output exp/baseline_cla/cla2_ruler_4k_seed42.json
steps.step3: Run CLA-4 on Llama-3.1-8B (4x compression): CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python exp/baseline_cla/run.py --model meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct --group_size 4 --benchmark ruler --seq_len 4096 --seed 42 --output exp/baseline_cla/cla4_ruler_4k_seed42.json
steps.step4: Run CLA variants on RULER 8K: Repeat with --seq_len 8192
steps.step5: Run CLA on LongBench: CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python exp/baseline_cla/run.py --model meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct --group_size 2 --benchmark longbench --seed 42 --output exp/baseline_cla/cla2_longbench_seed42.json
steps.step6: Repeat for seeds 43, 44: Run all configurations with different seeds
steps.step7: Aggregate CLA results: python scripts/aggregate_results.py --input_dir exp/baseline_cla/ --output exp/baseline_cla/results.json

title: Baseline 3 - xKV (CKA-Based Similarity, Strong Baseline)
description: Implement xKV baseline using CKA for similarity analysis and SVD-based compression. This is the STRONG baseline that addresses feedback - must show how we differ from and improve upon xKV.

steps.step1: Implement CKA computation: Create exp/baseline_xkv/cka.py with centered kernel alignment for KV cache similarity
steps.step2: Implement xKV-style SVD compression: Create exp/baseline_xkv/xkv_compression.py with cross-layer SVD per xKV paper
steps.step3: Profile CKA on subset of calibration data (128 samples for speed): CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python exp/baseline_xkv/profile_cka.py --model meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct --calibration data/calibration/calibration_1k.jsonl --n_samples 128 --output exp/baseline_xkv/cka_profile.json
steps.step4: Run xKV 2x compression on RULER 4K, 8K: CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python exp/baseline_xkv/run.py --model meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct --compression 2 --benchmark ruler --seq_len 4096 --seed 42 --output exp/baseline_xkv/xkv2x_ruler_4k_seed42.json
steps.step5: Run xKV 4x compression on RULER 4K: CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python exp/baseline_xkv/run.py --model meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct --compression 4 --benchmark ruler --seq_len 4096 --seed 42 --output exp/baseline_xkv/xkv4x_ruler_4k_seed42.json
steps.step6: Run xKV 2x on LongBench: Single config for comparison
steps.step7: Repeat key configs for seeds 43, 44 (not all variants)
steps.step8: Analyze CKA vs Query-KV patterns: Generate comparison showing CKA captures KV-KV similarity while our method captures Query-KV usage
steps.step9: Aggregate xKV results: python scripts/aggregate_results.py --input_dir exp/baseline_xkv/ --output exp/baseline_xkv/results.json

notes: CRITICAL: This baseline directly addresses xKV overlap feedback. We must show clear improvement over CKA/SVD approach.

title: Phase 1 - Build Query-KV Dependency Graph
description: Profile model on calibration data to construct Query-KV Dependency Graph. This is our core innovation - capturing how queries actually use KV caches.

steps.step1: Implement dependency graph construction: Create exp/layerselect/graph_builder.py
steps.step2: Implement cross-layer attention measurement: For each query layer i and KV layer j, compute average attention weight when layer i uses KV from layer j
steps.step3: Profile Llama-3.1-8B: CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python exp/layerselect/build_graph.py --model meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct --calibration data/calibration/calibration_1k.jsonl --seq_len 4096 --output exp/layerselect/dependency_graph_llama31_8b.npz
steps.step4: Profile Qwen2.5-7B: CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python exp/layerselect/build_graph.py --model Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct --calibration data/calibration/calibration_1k.jsonl --seq_len 4096 --output exp/layerselect/dependency_graph_qwen25_7b.npz
steps.step5: Visualize dependency matrices: python exp/layerselect/visualize_graph.py --input exp/layerselect/dependency_graph_llama31_8b.npz --output figures/dependency_matrix_llama31.pdf
steps.step6: Compute graph statistics: coverage scores, centrality measures, layer importance rankings
steps.step7: Compare dependency patterns between models (for generalization analysis)

notes: Profiling takes ~10-15 min per model as stated in proposal. Can run models in parallel on same GPU if memory allows.

title: Phase 2 - Multi-Objective Hub Selection
description: Run NSGA-II optimization to select hub layers. Optimizes for coverage, diversity (addressing KVSharer finding), and attention alignment.

steps.step1: Implement multi-objective formulation: Create exp/layerselect/optimizer.py with pymoo NSGA-II
steps.step2: Define objective functions: coverage(A, s), diversity(s, CKA_matrix), alignment(s, attention_divergence)
steps.step3: Run optimization for 2x compression: CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python exp/layerselect/optimize.py --dependency_graph exp/layerselect/dependency_graph_llama31_8b.npz --budget 16 --pop_size 100 --n_generations 200 --output exp/layerselect/hubs_2x_llama31.json
steps.step4: Run optimization for 4x compression: CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python exp/layerselect/optimize.py --dependency_graph exp/layerselect/dependency_graph_llama31_8b.npz --budget 8 --pop_size 100 --n_generations 200 --output exp/layerselect/hubs_4x_llama31.json
steps.step5: Generate Pareto frontier: Run optimization with varying budget weights to get tradeoff curve
steps.step6: Analyze selected hubs: python exp/layerselect/analyze_hubs.py --hubs exp/layerselect/hubs_2x_llama31.json --graph exp/layerselect/dependency_graph_llama31_8b.npz --output exp/layerselect/hub_analysis.json
steps.step8: Compare hub selection patterns across models

notes: NSGA-II runs on CPU, ~5-10 min per optimization. Pareto frontier requires multiple runs.

title: Phase 3 - LayerSelect Full Evaluation
description: Evaluate complete LayerSelect with attention-weighted dynamic routing. Main results comparing against baselines.

steps.step1: Implement attention-weighted routing: Create exp/layerselect/routing.py with dynamic aggregation
steps.step2: Implement full LayerSelect inference: Create exp/layerselect/run.py integrating graph, hubs, and routing
steps.step3: Run LayerSelect 2x on RULER 4K: CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python exp/layerselect/run.py --model meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct --hubs exp/layerselect/hubs_2x_llama31.json --benchmark ruler --seq_len 4096 --seed 42 --output exp/layerselect/layerselect2x_ruler_4k_seed42.json
steps.step4: Run LayerSelect 2x on RULER 8K: Similar command with --seq_len 8192
steps.step5: Run LayerSelect 4x on RULER 4K, 8K: Use hubs_4x_llama31.json
steps.step6: Run LayerSelect on LongBench: CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python exp/layerselect/run.py --model meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct --hubs exp/layerselect/hubs_2x_llama31.json --benchmark longbench --seed 42 --output exp/layerselect/layerselect2x_longbench_seed42.json
steps.step7: Measure inference throughput: Add --measure_throughput flag to record tokens/sec
steps.step9: Run on Qwen2.5-7B for cross-model validation
steps.step10: Aggregate LayerSelect results: python scripts/aggregate_results.py --input_dir exp/layerselect/ --output exp/layerselect/results.json

title: Ablation 1 - Component Ablation Study
description: Test each objective (coverage, diversity, alignment) independently to show contribution. Required for paper.

steps.step1: Coverage-only variant: Run NSGA-II with only coverage objective (no diversity, no alignment)
steps.step2: Diversity-only variant: Run NSGA-II with only diversity objective (no coverage, no alignment)
steps.step3: Alignment-only variant: Run NSGA-II with only alignment objective (no coverage, no diversity)
steps.step6: Evaluate all variants: Run each variant on RULER 4K with seed 42
steps.step7: Compare performance: Show that full three-objective outperforms all ablations
steps.step8: Generate ablation table: python scripts/generate_ablation_table.py --output figures/ablation_table.pdf

notes: Critical ablation showing each component contributes meaningfully (success criterion #3). Running subset of variants.

title: Ablation 2 - Graph Construction Comparison (xKV Overlap Analysis)
description: Directly compare Query-KV dependency (our method) vs KV-KV similarity (xKV-style). Addresses feedback about novelty over xKV.

steps.step1: Build KV-KV similarity graph: Use CKA to compute layer similarity (like xKV)
steps.step2: Run hub selection with KV-KV similarity: Same optimization, different input graph
steps.step3: Run hub selection with Query-KV dependency: Our proposed graph
steps.step4: Compare hub selections: Analyze overlap/difference in selected hubs
steps.step5: Evaluate both on RULER: Show accuracy difference at same compression
steps.step6: Generate comparison figure: Side-by-side visualization of CKA matrix vs Dependency matrix
steps.step7: Compute correlation: Measure how CKA similarity correlates with dependency scores
steps.step8: Key result: Demonstrate that Query-KV dependency provides additional signal beyond KV-KV similarity

notes: CRITICAL: This ablation addresses xKV feedback by showing Query-KV dependency outperforms CKA-based selection.

title: Ablation 3 - Routing Mechanism Comparison
description: Compare attention-weighted dynamic routing vs alternatives (direct copy, SVD, averaging).

steps.step1: Direct copy routing: Spoke layer directly uses hub KV cache (CLA-style)
steps.step2: Average routing: Simple average of multiple hub KVs
steps.step3: SVD reconstruction: xKV-style compression of hub KVs
steps.step4: Attention-weighted (ours): Dynamic aggregation based on dependency scores
steps.step5: Evaluate all routing methods: Same hub selection, different routing
steps.step6: Measure attention divergence: KL divergence between original and compressed attention patterns
steps.step7: Key result: Show attention-weighted routing has 30-50% lower divergence (success criterion #4)

notes: Testing key routing variants (direct copy, attention-weighted, SVD)

title: Ablation 4 - Budget Sensitivity Analysis
description: Test performance across different compression ratios (number of hubs).

steps.step1: Run with budget=24 (1.33x compression): CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python exp/layerselect/optimize.py --budget 24
steps.step2: Run with budget=16 (2x compression): Already done in main experiment
steps.step3: Run with budget=12 (2.67x compression): CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python exp/layerselect/optimize.py --budget 12
steps.step4: Run with budget=8 (4x compression): Already done in main experiment
steps.step5: Run with budget=6 (5.33x compression): CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python exp/layerselect/optimize.py --budget 6
steps.step7: Generate compression-accuracy curve: Plot accuracy vs compression ratio
steps.step8: Identify optimal operating point: Where accuracy drop < 1% with maximum compression

notes: Testing 4 key budget points (1.33x, 2x, 4x, 5.33x) for Pareto curve

title: Statistical Significance Testing
description: Perform statistical tests to confirm improvements are significant.

steps.step1: Load results from all experiments: baseline_full, baseline_cla, baseline_xkv, layerselect
steps.step2: Compute mean and std across seeds: For each method and metric
steps.step3: Perform paired t-tests: LayerSelect vs xKV, LayerSelect vs CLA
steps.step4: Compute effect sizes: Cohen's d for practical significance
steps.step5: Generate significance table: With p-values and confidence intervals
steps.step6: Verify success criterion 1: LayerSelect achieves >=2x compression with <2% accuracy drop
steps.step7: Verify success criterion 2: LayerSelect dominates on Pareto frontier
steps.step8: Document all results: Save to exp/statistical_tests/results.json

title: Cross-Model Generalization Validation
description: Verify method generalizes across different model architectures.

steps.step2: Analyze if optimal hub patterns are consistent across models
steps.step3: Check if relative improvements over baselines hold
steps.step4: Document generalization: Verify method doesn't fail on different architectures (contradicting refuting evidence #4)

title: Generate Paper Figures
description: Create publication-ready figures for the research paper.

steps.step1: Figure 1 - Method Overview: Visualize Query-KV Dependency Graph, hub selection, and routing
steps.step2: Figure 2 - Pareto Frontier: Accuracy vs Compression ratio for all methods
steps.step3: Figure 3 - Dependency Matrices: Comparison of Query-KV vs KV-KV (CKA) matrices
steps.step4: Figure 4 - Hub Selection Visualization: Which layers selected as hubs at different budgets
steps.step5: Figure 5 - Ablation Results: Component contribution bar chart
steps.step6: Figure 6 - Attention Divergence: KL divergence comparison across routing methods
steps.step7: Table 1 - Main Results: Comparison with baselines on RULER and LongBench
steps.step9: Save all figures: Output to figures/ directory in PDF and PNG formats

title: Latency Overhead Analysis
description: Verify that attention-weighted routing doesn't introduce unacceptable latency (refuting criterion #3).

steps.step1: Measure baseline inference time: Full KV cache, no routing overhead
steps.step2: Measure CLA inference time: Direct copy has minimal overhead
steps.step3: Measure xKV inference time: SVD reconstruction overhead
steps.step4: Measure LayerSelect inference time: Attention-weighted routing overhead

title: Memory Usage Profiling
description: Verify actual memory savings match theoretical compression ratios.

steps.step2: Compare across methods: Full, CLA, xKV, LayerSelect
steps.step3: Verify compression ratios: Actual memory vs theoretical
steps.step4: Generate memory breakdown: KV cache vs model weights vs activation
